folio.label: 125r
folio.l: As wel by werk , as by auctoritee
For gentil herte , kithetú gentillesse
I se wel ©e han , of my distresse
Compassiou~ , my faire Canacee
Of verray wo~manly , benygnetee
That nature , in ©oure principijs hatú set
But for non hope , for to fare the bet
But for to obeye , vn to ©oure herte free
And for to maken othere , I war by me
As by the whelp , is chastised the leouö
Rigút for that cause , and that conclusiouö
Whil that I haue a leiser , and a space
Myn harm I wil confessen , or I pace
And whil that oon , hire sorwe tolde
That other wep~ , as she to water wolde
Til that the faucou~ , bad hire to be stille
And witú a syke , rigút thus she seide hire wylle
¢ There I was bred , allas that ilke day
An forstred in a roche , of marbel gray
So tenderly , that no thyngö eyled me
I nyst nat , what was aduäsitee
Til I coude flee , ful heigú vnder the sky
Tho dwelled a Tercelet , me fast by
That semed wellewellwelle , of al gentillesse
Al were he ful of tresou~ , & falsnesse
It was so wrapped , vnder humble chere
And vnder hew of trouthe , in sucú manere
Vnder plesaunce , & vnder besy peyne
That no wygút wold han wend , he coude feyne
So diep in greyn , he dyed hise colours
Rigút as a serpent , hidetú vnder floures
Til he may sen his tyme , for to byte

note.notetxt: 505after this line change in the pen. Decrease in the size of writings. Double check.ODR

folio.l: Rigút so , this god of loues Ypocrite
Dotú his sämouns , and obeisaunces
And kepetú in semblaunt , alle his obseruaunces
That sovnetú in to gentillesse , of loue
As on a Toumbe , is al the fair a boue
And vnder is the cors , swicú as ©e wote
Swicú was this Ypocrite , bothe cold and hote
And in this wyse , he serued his entente
That saue the fend , noon wist what he ment
Til he so longe had weped , and compleyned
And many ©ere , his seruyse to me feyned
Til that myn herte , to pitous & to nyce
Al Innocent , of his crowned malice
So fered of his detú , as thougút me
